figures.caption: Table 1: Hyperparameters for digits generation experiments
Figure 2: Flow matching loss during training with GradEP. Loss decreases smoothly from 2.75 to 0.32 over 2000 epochs
Figure 3: 64 generated digit samples from 𝑡 = 0 to 𝑡 = 1. Most samples are clearly identifiable across all ten digit classes

Figure 1: GradEP mechanism. (a) Free phase: the spring potential (red, dashed) creates a minimum at 𝑥∗ near 𝑥𝑡 ; the displacement encodes the current velocity 𝑣 . (b) Nudged phase: the nudge loss pulls the equilibrium from 𝑥∗ toward 𝑥∗𝛽 , closer to the target velocity 𝑣 .
Figure 4: 64 generated digit samples from 𝑡 = 0 to 𝑡 = 1.2, showing sharper samples compared to 𝑡 = 1.0 (Figure 3)
